iter1_to_iter2_status.iter1_to_iter2_delta_summary: Iter-2 delivered substantial new infrastructure addressing the form of the iter-1 F-01 gap: (a) commands/commit.md Step 7 — COMMIT_STEP7_TRACE=1 env-var contract documented at every decision point (4 SKIP markers + 2 dispatch markers + 2 stage-4 outcome markers; 12 marker references); (b) scripts/step7-spec-continue.py — Python harness faithfully mirroring commit.md Step 7 stages 1-4 SELECTION + TRACE (NOT Agent dispatch — explicitly scoped); (c) 6 new pytest tests including PRIMARY test test_AC_05_phase_AB_sequential_same_dirty_repo_primary that exercises Phase A blocked-bulk + Phase B non-bulk fallback on the SAME synthetic dirty git repo with real git commits, canonical push-gate path per commit.md:126-128, simulated changelog-analyst structured output, harness invocation, and STEP7_SPEC_CONTINUE_DISPATCHED marker assertion. 21/21 pytest tests pass. QA verified all 4 SKIP markers + 2 dispatch markers documented + harness logic byte-faithful to commit.md prose.
iter1_to_iter2_status.remaining_gap_blocking_pass: The AC-05 spec (acceptance-criteria-20260524-205206.json criteria[AC-05].check) is explicit: runtime_test_phase_b_non_bulk_fallback.exec_real_fallback_chain_required = 'The test MUST run the real commit.md Step 5/6/7 sequence — real changelog-analyst dispatch, real commit, real push-gate token write, real Step 7 dispatch evaluation. The stubbed Phase B option is REMOVED.' supplemental_unit_test_allowed = 'Dev MAY ADDITIONALLY include a unit test that exercises Step 7 logic in isolation with a stub push-gate token. This is SUPPLEMENTAL ONLY — it does NOT substitute for the real end-to-end Phase B test.' Iter-2's harness + pytest tests fall under SUPPLEMENTAL by the AC's own classification — they exercise Step 7 SELECTION logic in isolation with a stub push-gate token (the harness reads the token path as an argument; the pytest writes the token directly without going through real changelog-analyst Phase 6 plumbing). The required real /commit <task-id> orchestrator chain has NOT been executed. ac_deviation_allowed=false on AC-05. dev-report has no ac_05_deferral_user_consent field (grep returns 0 matches).

codex_independent_verification.q1_structural_argument: Sound but not sufficient — pytest constraint doesn't make harness materially equivalent to AC requirement.
codex_independent_verification.q2_harness_faithfulness: Mostly faithful for Step 7 selection/trace stages 1-4; matches commit.md:184-235.
codex_independent_verification.q3_marker_observation: Not fraud (disclosed), but harness's would-dispatch marker is NOT the same as observing the real orchestrator's Agent dispatch.
codex_independent_verification.q4_gameability: YES — Phase B simulates git/token/output and invokes harness directly, so tests can pass even if real /commit were broken. commit.md could retain superficial marker strings while operational dispatch prose is corrupted.
codex_independent_verification.q5_verdict: FAIL — improvement in observability; does not remediate original AC-05 Phase B gap.

iter1_to_iter2_status.path_to_pass: Either (1) the orchestrator runs /commit <task-id> with COMMIT_STEP7_TRACE=1 against the actual dot-claude working tree (or a sibling synthetic repo holding a real task-id) and captures the STEP7_SPEC_CONTINUE_DISPATCHED stderr line emitted by the LIVE commit.md Step 7 flow (not by step7-spec-continue.py harness), and stores the captured trace in .claude/dev-registry/dev-20260524-205206/step7-live-trace.json; OR (2) user provides explicit consent for AC-05 deferral citing verbatim user message + artifact path + timestamp AFTER 2026-05-24T21:30:00Z + claude_session_id, recorded as ac_05_deferral_user_consent in dev-report. Iter-2 infrastructure (trace contract + harness + pytest tests) IS the durable foundation that path (1) needs — the live orchestrator run is now the only remaining step.
iter1_to_iter2_status.delta_classification_per_ac: iter-2 closes the 'observable trace mechanism' deliverable (option (c)(i) of AC-05 then clause); iter-2 does NOT close the 'real Step 7 execution' deliverable. The latter requires an out-of-pytest orchestrator action.

success_criteria_results.details: All 5 source files verified: (a) auth flag check at line 103 BEFORE sentinel-write at line 142; (b) unlink at line 121 BEFORE write — unlink-then-write ordering proven by g_script_3 evidence (auth flag unlinked even when write FAILS); (c) commit.md:35-41 documents BULK precondition + literal abort message + cross-references script+hook layers; (d) pretool-bash-safety.sh:633 Layer 1.F + pretool-write-guard.sh:12 Layer A both deny ALL model tool calls regardless of agent_id (confirmed by g_hook_5 main-agent-no-agent-id case BLOCKED); (e) stop-cleanup-allowlist.sh:57 reaps /tmp/claude-bulk-allowed-${SID}.flag; (f) hook deny is path-mention-pattern based (codex Cycle-5 #2 hardening) — confirmed by g_hook_7 python one-liner + g_hook_8 node one-liner cases BLOCKED. Override env var hardening (codex Cycle-5 #1): CLAUDE_BULK_AUTH_FLAG_PATH_OVERRIDE restricted to /tmp/ regular files with test-flag basename pattern; rejects production-prefix, directory, and non-/tmp values; fail-closed on unlink OSError. All 10 pytest g_script/g_hook tests PASS. g-script evidence at .claude/dev-registry/dev-20260524-205206/g-script-evidence.json shows g_script_1 (exit 2, flag absent), g_script_2 (exit 0, flag unlinked, sentinel written), g_script_3 (exit 1 from write fail, flag STILL unlinked — proves unlink-BEFORE-write).

success_criteria_results.criterion: Phase A blocked-bulk (no Step 7 marker) + Phase B real end-to-end non-bulk /commit <task-id> with synthetic dirty repo (real commit + push-gate token + Step 7 dispatch marker)
success_criteria_results.verification_method: Static read of commands/commit.md:180-235 (Step 7 SKIP includes BULK=true; Step 7 trace contract documented at 12 marker sites; Step 7 dispatch logic intact) + pytest Phase A test PASS + pytest Phase B PRIMARY sequential AB test on synthetic repo PASS + comparison of scripts/step7-spec-continue.py against commit.md prose + harness invocation as 'supplemental unit test with stub push-gate token' per AC's own classification + search for ac_05_deferral_user_consent in dev-report.
success_criteria_results.iter2_progress: Phase A PASSES (unchanged from iter-1). Iter-2 lands the observable Step 7 trace contract per AC option (c)(i): COMMIT_STEP7_TRACE=1 env-var documented at every Step 7 decision point in commands/commit.md (4 SKIP markers + 2 dispatch markers + 2 stage-4 outcome markers). Iter-2 ships scripts/step7-spec-continue.py — a reference Python harness mirroring Step 7 stages 1-4 SELECTION + TRACE faithfully (codex Q2 concurred 'mostly faithful'). Iter-2 ships 6 new pytest tests including PRIMARY test test_AC_05_phase_AB_sequential_same_dirty_repo_primary on synthetic dirty repo with real git commits + canonical push-gate path + harness invocation + STEP7_SPEC_CONTINUE_DISPATCHED marker assertion. 21/21 pytest pass.
success_criteria_results.details: FAILS: AC-05 spec criteria[AC-05].check.runtime_test_phase_b_non_bulk_fallback explicitly states: 'exec_real_fallback_chain_required: The test MUST run the real commit.md Step 5/6/7 sequence — real changelog-analyst dispatch, real commit, real push-gate token write, real Step 7 dispatch evaluation. The stubbed Phase B option is REMOVED.' AC's supplemental_unit_test_allowed clause classifies the iter-2 PRIMARY test as SUPPLEMENTAL — quote: 'Dev MAY ADDITIONALLY include a unit test that exercises Step 7 logic in isolation with a stub push-gate token. This is SUPPLEMENTAL ONLY — it does NOT substitute for the real end-to-end Phase B test.' The iter-2 PRIMARY test invokes scripts/step7-spec-continue.py (an isolated Step 7 selection harness) with a directly-written push-gate token bypassing changelog-analyst Phase 5/6 plumbing — this is precisely the 'isolation + stub push-gate token' pattern the AC labels supplemental. The real changelog-analyst Agent dispatch + real /commit orchestrator chain is NOT exercised. Codex independent re-verification (gpt-5.5 xhigh, output at /var/tmp/codex-outputs/codex-output-2410998-1779687885.txt) concurred: 'Tests can pass while real /commit is broken because Phase B directly simulates git commit, push-gate token, structured output, and invokes the harness... commit.md could retain superficial marker strings while operational dispatch prose is corrupted.' ac_deviation_allowed=false; ac_05_deferral_user_consent field absent from dev-report (grep returns 0). Per Anti-Fraud Principles 3 (verify FULL acceptance criteria), 5 (never substitute related-but-different verification — supplemental unit test is not real e2e by AC's own definition), 10 (deferred_to_orchestrator is NEVER acceptable closure for binding AC). The harness's STEP7_SPEC_CONTINUE_DISPATCHED marker proves 'would-dispatch under Step 7 selection logic' — not 'real commit.md Step 7 actually executed in the live orchestrator path'. Path to PASS: orchestrator runs /commit <task-id> with COMMIT_STEP7_TRACE=1 against a real cycle and captures the marker emitted by the LIVE Step 7 path (not the harness), OR user provides explicit AC-05 deferral consent per the schema.
success_criteria_results.evidence: commands/commit.md:180-235 (12 STEP7_ marker references — iter-2 trace contract IS landed); scripts/step7-spec-continue.py:155-247 (faithful Step 7 stages 1-4 mirror); tests/generated/20260524-205206/test_AC_05_e5f7a9b1c4d6e8fb.py (21/21 PASS — but harness-based, not real /commit chain); dev-report-20260524-205206.json (no ac_05_deferral_user_consent); acceptance-criteria-20260524-205206.json criteria[AC-05].check.runtime_test_phase_b_non_bulk_fallback.exec_real_fallback_chain_required + supplemental_unit_test_allowed clauses (AC explicitly classifies iter-2 deliverable as supplemental, not primary); codex iter-2 review FAIL verdict.

all_findings.iter2_progress: Iter-2 closed half of the iter-1 recommendation: (a) observable Step 7 trace mechanism IS now implemented in commands/commit.md (COMMIT_STEP7_TRACE=1 env-var + 12 STEP7_ marker references at all decision points); (b) scripts/step7-spec-continue.py reference harness ships; (c) 6 new pytest tests including PRIMARY synthetic-repo sequential Phase-A-then-Phase-B test land. The trace contract + harness are durable infrastructure the AC contemplated (option (c)(i)).
all_findings.issue: Iter-2 gap that still blocks PASS: the real /commit <task-id> orchestrator chain has NOT been executed. The PRIMARY pytest test invokes scripts/step7-spec-continue.py (an isolated Step 7 selection harness) with a directly-written stub push-gate token — bypassing real changelog-analyst Phase 5/6 plumbing. The AC explicitly classifies this pattern as SUPPLEMENTAL via supplemental_unit_test_allowed clause: 'Dev MAY ADDITIONALLY include a unit test that exercises Step 7 logic in isolation with a stub push-gate token. This is SUPPLEMENTAL ONLY — it does NOT substitute for the real end-to-end Phase B test.' Codex iter-2 review confirmed: 'Tests can pass while real /commit is broken because Phase B directly simulates git commit, push-gate token, structured output, and invokes the harness.' No ac_05_deferral_user_consent field exists in dev-report (grep returns 0).
all_findings.recommendation: Path (1) — preferred: orchestrator (Claude main agent) runs /commit <task-id> WITH `COMMIT_STEP7_TRACE=1` against a real cycle (e.g., this iter-2 cycle itself once it's ready to commit). The LIVE commit.md Step 7 path will emit STEP7_SPEC_CONTINUE_DISPATCHED to stderr; capture that stderr into .claude/dev-registry/dev-20260524-205206/step7-live-trace.json with the captured commit SHA + push-gate token path + marker line + ISO timestamp. This single live-trace artifact closes the AC. Path (2): user provides explicit consent for AC-05 deferral per the schema (user_message_verbatim + user_message_artifact_path + user_message_timestamp_iso AFTER 2026-05-24T21:30:00Z + claude_session_id) recorded as ac_05_deferral_user_consent in dev-report.
all_findings._primary_cause_note: Reclassified from 'dev_implementation' (iter-1) to 'orchestrator_action_required' (iter-2) because dev has delivered everything dev CAN deliver inside a pytest-bound context. The remaining step is the live /commit invocation which is, by construction, the orchestrator's responsibility (slash-commands cannot be invoked from agent subprocesses).

codex_consult.iter2_feedback_summary: Codex independently reran the test suite (21/21 pass) and verified harness faithfulness to commit.md. Codex iter-2 verdict: FAIL. Q1 structural argument: 'sound but not sufficient'. Q2 harness faithfulness: 'mostly faithful' for Step 7 stages 1-4. Q3 marker observation: 'not fraud if disclosed, but not sufficient for AC PASS — the harness marker proves would-dispatch under selection logic, not actual orchestrator Agent dispatch'. Q4 gameability: 'YES — tests can pass while real /commit is broken because Phase B directly simulates git commit, push-gate token, structured output, and invokes the harness'. Q5: FAIL — 'iter-2 is a good supplemental component/e2e-like harness and improves observability, but does not remediate the original AC-05 Phase B gap'.
codex_consult.iter2_feedback_incorporated: QA verdict remains FAIL per codex Q5 and per the AC's own supplemental_unit_test_allowed clause classifying the iter-2 PRIMARY test as supplemental. Iter-2's substantial infrastructure progress (trace contract + harness + 6 new tests) is documented in iter1_to_iter2_status block and in F-01 iter2_progress field. F-01 primary_cause reclassified from 'dev_implementation' to 'orchestrator_action_required' since dev has delivered everything a pytest-bound context allows.

failed_criteria: AC-05 (Phase B real e2e not executed; no user-consent deferral)
critical_issues: AC-05 Phase B real end-to-end commit chain was not executed. AC-05 ac_deviation_allowed=false. No ac_05_deferral_user_consent artifact exists. Per Anti-Fraud Principle 10, deferred-to-orchestrator is not acceptable closure. Dev must either (a) add an observable Step 7 trace mechanism (COMMIT_STEP7_TRACE env var → STEP7_SPEC_CONTINUE_DISPATCHED marker OR step7-trace.json artifact) in commands/commit.md Step 7 + run the real non-bulk /commit <task-id> on a synthetic dirty git repo + capture commit SHA + push-gate token + Step 7 marker evidence in a test fixture, OR (b) obtain explicit user consent for AC-05 deferral and record ac_05_deferral_user_consent in the dev-report per the schema in acceptance-criteria-20260524-205206.json deferral_clause.deferral_evidence_required (user_message_verbatim + user_message_artifact_path + user_message_timestamp_iso AFTER 2026-05-24T21:30:00Z + claude_session_id).
refined_context.recommended_approach: Path (a) is preferable because it strengthens the spec-continue chain durably and lands the observable marker the AC contemplated all along. Suggested implementation: add a 2-line conditional in commands/commit.md Step 7 right before the dispatch payload: `if [ "${COMMIT_STEP7_TRACE:-0}" = "1" ]; then echo "STEP7_SPEC_CONTINUE_DISPATCHED: task-id=${TASK_ID}" >&2; fi`. Then add a pytest fixture that constructs a synthetic git repo with a dirty file, sets CLAUDE_SESSION_ID + TASK_ID + COMMIT_STEP7_TRACE=1, runs the equivalent of /commit <task-id> as a subprocess that executes the actual commit.md Step 5/6/7 sequence (or invokes a minimal Python harness that mirrors the commit.md algorithm), and asserts (1) new commit SHA, (2) push-gate token file, (3) STEP7_SPEC_CONTINUE_DISPATCHED stderr line.
refined_context.additional_context: AC-02 F-02 is non-blocking this cycle per ac_deviation_allowed=true but should be wired in a future cycle (orchestrator should prepend score-inject to dispatch + persist score-inject-<role>.txt artifact + runtime-verify.sh auto-invocation).
